channel.title: The Ganja Club
channel.description: Medical cannabis news, policy analysis, and culture.

item.title: Best Vaporisers for UK Medical Cannabis Patients (2026): The 5 That Actually Deliver
item.description: From the clinic-grade Mighty+ to the budget-friendly XMAX V3 Pro, we tested the five dry-herb vaporisers that UK patients actually rate. Not a single conduction pen made the cut.

item.title: Medical Cannabis in Birmingham (2026): Clinics, Eligibility & What Patients Should Know
item.description: Birmingham is the UK's second city, and its residents have full access to every UK cannabis clinic. Here is how the system works, which clinics serve the Midlands, and what your first step should be.

item.title: Medical Cannabis in London (2026): Clinics, Eligibility & What Patients Should Know
item.description: London has more prescribing clinics than anywhere else in the UK — but most Londoners still do not know medical cannabis is legal. Here is how the system works in the capital, which clinics serve London patients, and what your first step should be.

item.title: Medical Cannabis in Manchester (2026): Clinics, Eligibility & What Patients Should Know
item.description: Manchester patients can access every UK cannabis clinic via video consultation — no travel required. Here is how the system works, which clinics best serve the North West, and what your first step should be.

item.title: Europe Turns Green: How EU Cannabis Reform Momentum Is Reshaping the Continent
item.description: From German pilot programmes to Czech legalisation and French medical trials, European cannabis reform is accelerating. We examine the patchwork of policies reshaping access across the continent.

item.title: Bridging the Gap: Why Medical Cannabis Access Still Fails Patients Who Need It Most
item.description: Eight years after legalisation, the gap between policy and practice in UK medical cannabis has never been wider. We examine the cost, geography, and bureaucracy that keep patients from the treatment they are legally entitled to.

item.title: UK Cannabis Licensing Reform: What the 2026 Home Office Review Means for Patients
item.description: The Home Office has launched a review of medical cannabis licensing that could reshape how UK patients access treatment. Here is what the proposed reforms mean for patients, clinics, and the future of medical cannabis in Britain.

item.title: The Daily Briefing — 11 June 2026
item.description: Federal marijuana trafficking prosecutions in US fall to historic low New data from the United States shows that federal law enforcement officials charged fewer than 400 people with marijuana

item.title: The Daily Briefing — 5 June 2026
item.description: Alabama patients finally gain access to authorised medical cannabis after years of delays Patients in Alabama can now legally purchase authorised medical cannabis products, following years of

item.title: The Daily Briefing — 4 June 2026
item.description: Kentucky governor signs executive order expanding medical cannabis access Governor Andy Beshear has issued an emergency executive order expanding access to medical cannabis in Kentucky. The order,

item.title: The Daily Briefing — 3 June 2026
item.description: Kentucky Governor expands medical cannabis access via executive order Governor Andy Beshear has issued an executive order expanding medical cannabis access in Kentucky. The emergency regulation is

item.title: The Daily Briefing — 2 June 2026
item.description: Global cancer workforce shortage raises concerns for patient access to care A new report from researchers published by The Guardian warns that the world faces a cancer workforce crisis, with a

item.title: The Daily Briefing — 31 May 2026
item.description: New analysis finds opioid overdoses decline after cannabis legalisation A fresh analysis published this week by NORML has added to the growing body of evidence that legal cannabis access is

item.title: The Daily Briefing — 30 May 2026
item.description: Analysis finds opioid overdose rates decline after cannabis legalisation A new analysis published this week adds to the growing body of evidence linking legal cannabis access to reductions in

item.title: The Daily Briefing — 29 May 2026
item.description: Pew Poll finds majority of Americans now oppose cannabis prohibition A new survey from the Pew Research Centre has found that support for ending cannabis prohibition continues to grow among the

item.title: Cannabis Europa 2026: The Continent Accelerates as the UK Stands Still
item.description: At Cannabis Europa London 2026, held in May, delegates confronted a stark divide: the EU races toward regulatory harmonisation and adult-use reform, while the UK remains mired in a stalled medical programme. Investment is flowing east, and the conversation has shifted from ‘if’ to ‘when’ for adult-use in countries like the Czech Republic and the Netherlands.

item.title: The Daily Briefing — 28 May 2026
item.description: Pew Poll Shows Strong Public Support for Ending Marijuana Prohibition in US A new poll from the Pew Research Center has found that marijuana prohibition is increasingly unpopular among Americans. The

item.title: The Daily Briefing — 27 May 2026
item.description: Pew poll finds broad US public support for ending cannabis prohibition A new survey from the Pew Research Center has found that marijuana prohibition is increasingly unpopular among American voters.

item.title: The Daily Briefing — 26 May 2026
item.description: NORML seeks representation at DEA rescheduling hearing The National Organisation for the Reform of Marijuana Laws (NORML) has formally requested a seat at the upcoming Drug Enforcement Administration

item.title: The Daily Briefing — 25 May 2026
item.description: NORML calls for renewed support in fight for cannabis justice The US advocacy organisation NORML has issued a public appeal for continued support, arguing that the fight for "justice, equity, and

item.title: The Daily Briefing — 24 May 2026
item.description: Amber heat health alerts issued for UK bank holiday weekend The UK Health Security Agency has issued amber heat health alerts for the bank holiday weekend, with forecasters predicting record-breaking

item.title: The Daily Briefing — 23 May 2026
item.description: US House passes funding bill to expand medical cannabis access for veterans The US House of Representatives has approved an FY27 funding bill that includes a provision to expand medical cannabis

item.title: The Daily Briefing — 22 May 2026
item.description: House-approved funding bill secures medical cannabis access for US veterans The US House of Representatives has approved a Fiscal Year 2027 funding bill that includes a provision protecting medical

item.title: The Daily Briefing — 21 May 2026
item.description: House-approved US funding bill includes medical cannabis access for veterans The US House of Representatives has approved a Fiscal Year 2027 funding bill that includes a provision allowing veterans

item.title: The Daily Briefing — 18 May 2026
item.description: UK reform outlook, the Guardian's deep dive on access barriers, and Cannabis Europa London kicks off next week.

item.title: CQC Concerns Add to Scrutiny of UK Private Medical Cannabis Clinics
item.description: The Care Quality Commission has raised concerns about the standard of care in private medical cannabis clinics. Here's what it means for patients.

item.title: The UK Medical Cannabis Market: Growth, Challenges and the Road Ahead
item.description: The UK medical cannabis market is expanding rapidly, but significant barriers remain for patients. A comprehensive look at where things stand in May 2026.

item.title: Patient Experiences: Navigating Stigma in the UK Healthcare System
item.description: Patients report that stigma around cannabis as medicine remains one of the biggest challenges — often within the healthcare system itself.

item.title: Green Gold Rush: US Medical Cannabis Market on Track to Surpass $45 Billion by 2027, Q1 2026 Report Reveals
item.description: Q1 2026 data projects US medical cannabis market to exceed $45 billion by 2027, with record sales but price compression squeezing multi-state operators in mature western states.

item.title: Global Cannabis Reform Roundup: Policy Changes Across the World
item.description: From Germany to Brazil, the global cannabis reform landscape is shifting rapidly. A roundup of the major policy developments you need to know.

item.title: Welcome to The Ganja Club — The Story So Far
item.description: Why we're here, what we stand for, and what you can expect from this publication. Medical cannabis news, analysis, and the culture that connects the community.

item.title: Inside the UK's Underground Cannabis Community: A New Kind of Culture
item.description: Beyond the clinics and the headlines, a new cannabis community is taking shape across the UK — one built on education, peer support, and shared experience.

item.title: Key Cannabinoid Research Studies to Watch in 2026
item.description: From pain management to mental health, the clinical research pipeline for cannabinoid medicines is more active than ever. Here are the studies to watch.

item.title: NHS Prescribing: Why Medical Cannabis Remains Out of Reach for Most Patients
item.description: Eight years after the law changed, NHS prescribing of cannabis-based medicines remains negligible. Here's what's blocking progress.

item.title: What the UK Can Learn From Germany's Medical Cannabis Model
item.description: Germany's medical cannabis system has expanded rapidly since 2024 reforms. The UK could learn valuable lessons from its European neighbour.

item.title: CBD for Anxiety: What the Latest Clinical Trials Tell Us
item.description: New clinical trials are providing clearer evidence on CBD's potential for anxiety disorders. Here's what the science says in May 2026.

item.title: Patient Rights Under Threat: The Campaign for NHS Access Continues
item.description: Patient advocacy groups are ramping up their campaign for equal access to cannabis-based medicines on the NHS.

item.title: Culture Shift: How London's Cannabis Scene Is Evolving
item.description: From private members' clubs to patient meetups, London's cannabis culture is maturing. A look at the community behind the medicine.

item.title: THC for Chronic Pain: New Evidence Strengthens the Case
item.description: A major new meta-analysis provides some of the strongest evidence yet for THC-based treatments in chronic pain management.

item.title: European Medical Cannabis Market Breaks €2 Billion Barrier in 2026
item.description: The European medical cannabis market has surpassed €2 billion in annual revenue for the first time, driven by surging demand in Germany, the UK, Poland, and Italy. With domestic cultivation scaling up and insurance reimbursement expanding, the sector is poised for continued growth into 2027.

item.title: Home Office Review: Why Cannabis Scheduling Remains a Political Flashpoint
item.description: The Home Office's position on cannabis scheduling continues to draw criticism from clinicians, researchers, and patient groups.

item.title: How to Access Medical Cannabis Through a Private UK Clinic
item.description: A practical guide to accessing medical cannabis through a private UK clinic — what to expect, how much it costs, and how to choose a clinic.

item.title: Beyond THC and CBD: The Emerging Science of Terpenes
item.description: Terpenes — the aromatic compounds in cannabis — are emerging as a key area of research with potential therapeutic applications.

item.title: First Light in the First State: Delaware Launches Adult-Use Cannabis Sales, Becoming 25th Market
item.description: Delaware became the 25th US state to legalise adult-use cannabis sales on 15 April 2026, with three retailers opening under a social equity-focused licensing programme.

item.title: Germany’s Cannabis Experiment: One Year On from the Regional Pilot Programmes
item.description: A year after Germany launched its second pillar of adult-use cannabis reform, the regional pilot programmes are yielding mixed results. Home cultivation and social clubs have found their feet, but commercial supply chains face regulatory hurdles that offer key lessons for the rest of Europe.

item.title: Landmark Bipartisan Bill Aims to Expand Medical Cannabis Access for US Veterans
item.description: Bipartisan legislation and a $25 million VA funding push could finally allow doctors to recommend medical cannabis to veterans in legal states.

item.title: Prague’s Green Signal: Czech Republic Charts Its Own Path to Adult-Use Cannabis Regulation
item.description: In early 2026, the Czech Republic unveiled draft legislation for a regulated adult-use cannabis market, drawing inspiration from Germany’s model while tailoring provisions to local priorities. The move, paired with a major expansion of the medical programme, is reshaping Central Europe’s cannabis landscape and setting a potential timeline for legal sales by late 2027.

item.title: SAFE Banking Act Revived in US Senate with Bipartisan Push and 280E Reform Amendment
item.description: The SAFE Banking Act has been reintroduced in the US Senate with a key Section 280E reform amendment, advancing through committee hearings with bipartisan support.

item.title: FDA Unveils Long-Awaited CBD Regulatory Framework, Sparking Industry Debate
item.description: FDA releases March 2026 draft guidance on hemp-derived CBD, proposing a 20 mg daily cap and strict labelling rules for a $4B+ market.

item.title: DEA Cannabis Rescheduling Hearings Begin: Historic Shift Looms as Schedule III Debate Intensifies
item.description: DEA hearings in March 2026 weigh historic move of cannabis to Schedule III, with HHS and DEA officials clashing over medical use and public health risks.
